seed.title: Starship launch-cost collapse
seed.prompt: Given the thesis that reusable heavy launch makes mass-to-orbit much cheaper, generate consequences and select under explore and proof.
seed.thesis: Reusable heavy-lift launch changes space economics by making mass, volume, replacement cadence, and orbital construction less scarce.
goals: test a cross-domain transfer heavy seed
show launch-cost knock-ons without flat cascade
prove generation-2 lineage can sharpen a parent

operators.label: Substrate removal
operators.description: Find the launch constraint that weakens.

operators.label: Hidden dependent
operators.description: Find actors dependent on launch scarcity.

operators.label: Infrastructure inversion
operators.description: Ask whether cheap mass-to-orbit creates infrastructure for another market.

sourcePackets.title: Mass budget removal
sourcePackets.mechanism: If launch mass is cheaper, spacecraft can use heavier shielding, redundancy, and simpler commodity parts instead of extreme miniaturization.

candidate.title: Space hardware gets less precious
candidate.thesis: Cheap heavy launch moves spacecraft design from exquisite mass optimization toward heavier, redundant, serviceable hardware.

delta.summary: Moved from cheaper launch to changed hardware design rules.

claims: Lower mass penalty changes design tradeoffs before it changes final markets.
Commodity redundancy becomes cheaper than extreme miniaturization.

sourcePackets.title: Orbital construction yard
sourcePackets.mechanism: Cheap volume and cadence can turn orbit from destination into construction site for platforms too large to launch whole.

candidate.title: Orbit becomes a construction yard
candidate.thesis: Starship-class cadence could make orbital assembly an infrastructure business, where parts, fuel, and servicing create a construction supply chain above Earth.

delta.summary: Moved from transportation cost to orbit as a construction substrate.

claims: Large structures become possible when parts can be launched repeatedly.
The transfer maps shipyard logic onto orbital assembly.

sourcePackets.title: Insurance and replacement cadence
sourcePackets.mechanism: If satellites are cheaper to replace, the economics of insurance, qualification, and conservative lifetime design shift.

candidate.title: Replacement beats perfection
candidate.thesis: Cheap heavy launch lets some orbital businesses replace hardware often instead of over-insuring and over-qualifying every asset for long life.

delta.summary: Moved from launch cost to asset-lifecycle economics.

claims: Lower replacement cost changes insurance and qualification incentives.
Some platforms can trade longevity for update cadence.

sourcePackets.title: Rockets get cheaper
sourcePackets.mechanism: Restates the seed as cheaper launch without a new market or mechanism.
sourcePackets.noDeltaReason: No delta beyond the seed launch-cost thesis.

sourcePackets.title: Orbital dry dock
sourcePackets.mechanism: If orbit becomes a construction yard, the next dependency is a dry-dock layer for inspection, repair, refueling, and standardized attachment.

candidate.title: Orbital construction needs dry docks
candidate.thesis: The construction-yard thesis sharpens into orbital dry docks: reusable heavy launch is not enough unless platforms can be inspected, refueled, repaired, and mechanically standardized.

delta.summary: Moved from orbital construction to the service infrastructure that makes construction repeatable.

claims: Shipyard-style construction requires maintenance and standard attachment points.
The same infrastructure supports inspection, repair, and refueling.
